question_text: A hollow aluminum sphere has an outer diameter of 20 cm and a mass of 2160 g. When placed in water, it floats with half of its volume submerged. Use the density of water (1 g/cm³) to explain this observation and calculate what the average density of the sphere must be.
correct_answer: Volume of sphere ≈ 4189 cm³; average density = 0.515 g/cm³; it floats because this is less than water's density (1 g/cm³)
explanation: Using V = (4/3)πr³ with r = 10 cm gives volume ≈ 4189 cm³. Density = 2160 ÷ 4189 ≈ 0.515 g/cm³. The sphere floats because its average density (including the air inside) is less than water's density—the air spaces make the overall average less dense than water.
